front: What is a global state in a distributed system?
back: The global state of a distributed system is the set of all local states of its processes, combined with the state of the communication channels (i.e., the set of messages in transit).

front: Why is observing a true global state in a distributed system difficult?
back: Because of the lack of a global clock and the fact that processes are spatially separated. It is impossible to collect the state of all processes and channels at a single, precise instant in time due to communication delays.

front: What is a consistent global state (or consistent cut)?
back: A global state is consistent if it reflects a set of local states and channel states that could have occurred during the same execution of the system. If a receive event is recorded in a local state, the corresponding send event must also be recorded and precede it.

front: What is the Chandy-Lamport algorithm used for?
back: It is a snapshot algorithm used to record a consistent global state of a distributed system. It uses special marker messages to coordinate the recording of process states and the state of channels without freezing the entire system.

front: What is an inconsistent global state?
back: An inconsistent global state is one that could never have occurred in a real execution. A classic example is recording a state where a process has received a message, but the state of the sending process does not reflect that the message was ever sent.
